canonical_value: In an N-bit flash ADC, the analog voltage is fed simultaneously to \(2^N - 1\) comparators.
surface_forms: In an N-bit flash ADC, the analog voltage is fed simultaneously to \(2^N - 1\) comparators.

normalized_value.text: In an N-bit flash ADC, the analog voltage is fed simultaneously to \(2^N - 1\) comparators.

canonical_value: Assume that the analog voltage source \(V_{\text{in}}\) (whose output is being converted to digital format) has a source resistance of 75 \(\Omega\), and the input capacitance of each comparator is 8 pF, as shown in the circuit diagram.
surface_forms: Assume that the analog voltage source \(V_{\text{in}}\) (whose output is being converted to digital format) has a source resistance of 75 \(\Omega\), and the input capacitance of each comparator is 8 pF, as shown in the circuit diagram.

canonical_value: The input must settle to an accuracy of 1/2 LSB even for a full-scale input change for proper conversion.

canonical_value: If the flash ADC has 8-bit resolution, what is the maximum sampling rate approximately?
surface_forms: If the flash ADC has 8-bit resolution, what is the maximum sampling rate approximately?

normalized_value.target_text: If the flash ADC has 8-bit resolution, what is the maximum sampling rate approximately?

canonical_value:
In an N-bit flash ADC, the analog voltage is fed simultaneously to \(2^N - 1\) comparators. The output of the comparators is then encoded to a binary format using digital circuits. Assume that the analog voltage source \(V_{\text{in}}\) has a source resistance of 75 \(\Omega\), and the input capacitance of each comparator is 8 pF. The input must settle to an accuracy of 1/2 LSB even for a full-scale input change for proper conversion. Assume that the time taken by the thermometer-to-binary encoder is negligible.

If the flash ADC has 8-bit resolution, what is the maximum sampling rate approximately?
surface_forms:
In an N-bit flash ADC, the analog voltage is fed simultaneously to \(2^N - 1\) comparators. The output of the comparators is then encoded to a binary format using digital circuits. Assume that the analog voltage source \(V_{\text{in}}\) has a source resistance of 75 \(\Omega\), and the input capacitance of each comparator is 8 pF. The input must settle to an accuracy of 1/2 LSB even for a full-scale input change for proper conversion. Assume that the time taken by the thermometer-to-binary encoder is negligible.

If the flash ADC has 8-bit resolution, what is the maximum sampling rate approximately?

normalized_value.title: Dataset 开放题
normalized_value.rewritten_question_text:
In an N-bit flash ADC, the analog voltage is fed simultaneously to \(2^N - 1\) comparators. The output of the comparators is then encoded to a binary format using digital circuits. Assume that the analog voltage source \(V_{\text{in}}\) has a source resistance of 75 \(\Omega\), and the input capacitance of each comparator is 8 pF. The input must settle to an accuracy of 1/2 LSB even for a full-scale input change for proper conversion. Assume that the time taken by the thermometer-to-binary encoder is negligible.

If the flash ADC has 8-bit resolution, what is the maximum sampling rate approximately?
